answer:
The procedure incorrectly resets `foundIndex` to 0 in the `ELSE` block for every name that does not match, so if the target is found but not in the last position, it will be overwritten to 0 by subsequent non-matching names.  
The only way to demonstrate this failure is to call the procedure with a target that appears somewhere in the list but not at the very end.  
Among the options, the target "Ben" appears in the middle of the list in option G, so the procedure will incorrectly display 0 instead of the correct index.

\boxed{G}
